### Session e4adfe27-7dfd-4b28-a086-6072194933d0 - 2026-07-14T16:01:32.141Z

- Spencer requested Claude Code to activate the Meeting Clips Lane: convert approved meeting moments into 9:16 captioned shorts for social posting, following the Podcast Shorts Lane pattern but using meeting clip plans instead of fresh selection.

- Claude attempted to query the Notion Meetings DB (data source `be8400c3-cbbe-43f8-bfd9-32f18730b153`) to find meetings marked "Clip for Social" == true, which is the privacy clearance gate for clipping private call recordings.

- The Notion Meetings DB returned 404 object_not_found via both available auth paths: the `.env` NOTION_TOKEN (scoped to Got Moles) and Spencer's claude.ai Notion connector (authenticated to GOT MOLES workspace, not Elevate 360).

- Claude correctly declined to bypass the privacy gate (the "Clip for Social" tick that clears third parties to be clipped) and did not attempt to process meetings found on disk as a fallback.

- Four hard infrastructure dependencies block this lane on the Got Moles install: (1) Notion Meetings DB access (Elevate 360 workspace), (2) Zoom credentials (ZOOM_ACCOUNT_ID/CLIENT_ID/CLIENT_SECRET absent from .env), (3) transcription API keys (ELEVENLABS_API_KEY and GROQ_API_KEY both absent), and (4) Video Intake inbox mount (G:\Shared drives\Elevate 360\24_Video Drops\Inbox\ unmounted).

- The scripts/meetings/ helpers and cron/jobs/meeting-clips-lane.md shipped in initial release but have never executed on this install; no projects/00-longform-to-shortform/_inbox/meetings/ directory or renders exist.

- Claude noted that all four blocking dependencies point to the Elevate 360 workspace side, where the Podcast Shorts Lane already runs; activating this lane requires infrastructure provisioning outside the Got Moles install.
